confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_c4a985ae-b3c5-4d18-8c61-d2962f6c2467 read back as promoted

proposal: On Windows, PC Site or User Site should own MCP process launch, client config, lifecycle supervision, and health checks for narada-mcp connections.
Target Sites should expose facade contracts and authority posture; they should not become implicit runtime supervisors by convenience.
Cross-Site MCP use should remain governed: execution machine Site launches the channel, target Site admits the consequence.
payload.recommendation: Route to narada-andrey User/PC Site policy and Narada proper docs as MCP runtime-locus guidance.
payload.summary: MCP facades should not imply that each target Site runs an always-on daemon. Narada proper defines MCP facade contracts, while the User/PC Site that hosts the local agent/client runtime should launch and supervise MCP server processes for addressed Sites.
payload.title: MCP runtime should be managed by top-level execution Site
